query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
## Summary

**The US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6.** On July 1, 2026, the US declined the optional 16-year extension under Article 34.7 — a distinct and much less severe action than a withdrawal notice.

**Key findings:**
- USTR Ambassador Jamieson Greer stated the US "did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form." Mexico and Canada both supported extension; since unanimity is required, the US non-concurrence triggered **annual reviews** under Article 34.7.4 (next deadline: July 1, 2027).
- **Article 34.7 (declining to extend) ≠ Article 34.6 (withdrawal).** Multiple authoritative sources (USTR, White & Case, Brownstein, CSIS, Brookings) confirm no withdrawal/termination procedure was invoked. Article 34.6 requires six months' written notice — none was issued.
- **USMCA remains fully in force.** The 16-year extension stays available "at any time" via written confirmation by the three heads of government. If never extended, the agreement expires July 1, 2036 — a decade-long runway, not imminent termination.
- **Next steps:** US–Mexico third bilateral round scheduled for week of July 20, 2026 in Mexico City; Canada had not yet begun substantive text negotiations.

I've delivered the full report above and submitted the structured findings.
